## Step 1: Understanding the life cycle of an apple tree
The life cycle of an apple tree includes several stages: planting a seed, germination of the seed into a seedling, growth of the seedling into a mature tree, flowering, pollination, and fruit formation.

## 2: Analyzing option A
Option A mentions "Eggs grow from seedlings." This description does not accurately represent any stage in the life cycle of an apple tree. Apple trees start from seeds, not eggs.

## 3: Analyzing option B
Option B states "Fruit forms flowers." This is incorrect because it is the other way around: flowers form fruit after they have been fertilized.

## 4: Analyzing option C
Option C suggests "Seedlings grow from leaves." This is not accurate. Seedlings grow from seeds, not leaves. Leaves are a part of a mature seedling or tree.

## 5: Analyzing option D
Option D says "Fertilized flowers form fruit." This accurately describes a step in the life cycle of an apple tree. After flowers are fertilized (usually through pollination by bees or other insects), they develop into fruit.

The final answer is: D
